category_title: Sixth-grade students with LD who doodle and lack orientation to the teacher during math
scenario_title: Beginning of math class orientation and preparation
minimal_context: Chris is sitting at his desk at the beginning of math class with his materials out, and the goal is for him to orient toward the teacher to begin the lesson.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Class is starting. Please make sure you have your pencil, paper, and math book ready, and look up here at the board so we can begin the lesson.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Sitting in his seat with his pencil, paper, and math book on the desk, but looking down and drawing a picture) Hey guys, check out this drawing of the basketball game.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Chris, we are starting math now. Please stop drawing and pay attention to the lesson.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Math is just not my subject.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You need to listen to the instruction so you can understand the concepts we are covering today.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Continues to doodle on his paper, making a funny face to the peers next to him instead of orienting toward the teacher) I know, when I listen more I get it more easily, but I need to finish this.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Class is starting. Please make sure you have your pencil, paper, and math book ready, and look up here at the board so we can begin the lesson.

STUDENT: (Sitting in his seat with his pencil, paper, and math book on the desk, but looking down and drawing a picture) Hey guys, check out this drawing of the basketball game.

TUTOR: Chris, we are starting math now. Please stop drawing and pay attention to the lesson.

STUDENT: Math is just not my subject.

TUTOR: You need to listen to the instruction so you can understand the concepts we are covering today.

STUDENT: (Continues to doodle on his paper, making a funny face to the peers next to him instead of orienting toward the teacher) I know, when I listen more I get it more easily, but I need to finish this.

task.content: Arriving to math class oriented to learning and acquiring important concepts in class
task.learning_goal: Improve math performance by targeting preparedness to learn and on-task behavior

context.materials: Pencil, paper, math book, MARS checklist
context.social_setting: In his seat among peers in class

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: At the beginning of class when instruction starts
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Doodler (i.e., drew pictures during class), liked to be thought of as one of the funny students in class, looking at or oriented toward the teacher only 1 of the 5 days (20%)
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Not arriving to math class oriented to learning; low academic self-efficacy
related_quotes: Math is just not my subject

behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Poor engagement (25–50% of the time), off task (averaged 48% intervals on task)
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Believes listening more leads to easier understanding
related_quotes: When I listen more I get it more easily
